Reverted this because it was causing linting failures.

* [Push with failures](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/jobs?repo=autoland&group_state=expanded&resultStatus=testfailed%2Cbusted%2Cexception&revision=578cedf096a6279346cb465efc5e31f1b8d4ef61&selectedTaskRun=CRwMfSifS5i_TXBGpuBS9Q.0&searchStr=Linting%2Copt%2CRust%2Cchecks%2Csource-test-vendor-rust%2Cvendor)

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Updating the neqo library to version 0.22.0 introduced linting failures that caused a revert before final successful push.
symptoms: Crash reports linked possibly related to memory allocation (OOM) in JS garbage collector
bug_analysis.technical_details: The bug involved updating the neqo library (a networking component related to QUIC) from an earlier version to v0.22.0/0.22.2. Initial push to v0.22.1 was reverted due to linting failures. The final update to v0.22.2 was accepted. There are linked crash reports mostly involving out-of-memory issues in the JavaScript garbage collector, but their direct relation to this bug is unclear.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Potential for build failures and delays in integrating the updated neqo library, possibly affecting networking performance or stability if the update is faulty.

common_patterns: Use-after-free or invalid memory access related to buffer management
Crashes triggered during neqo (QUIC) network operations after update
potential_root_causes: Race condition or improper synchronization in buffer handling within neqo
Incorrect memory management or lifetime issues in output buffer allocation
Incompatibility introduced by updating neqo to a new version causing invalid state transitions
relevant_fix_clues: No bugs currently show resolved status or explicit fixes
Summary mentions 'Update neqo to v0.22.0' indicating the crash appeared after upgrade
Crash location in get_output_buffer implies buffer retrieval or allocation logic is problematic
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crashes stem from buffer management issues introduced or exacerbated by the neqo update to v0.22.0. The crash signature in get_output_buffer points to invalid memory access likely due to race conditions or improper synchronization during buffer handling. No resolved bugs or fixes are currently available to confirm remediation strategies.

common_patterns: Use-after-free or invalid memory access related to buffer management
Crashes triggered during neqo (QUIC) network operations after update
potential_root_causes: Race condition or improper synchronization in buffer handling within neqo
Incorrect memory management or lifetime issues in output buffer allocation
Incompatibility introduced by updating neqo to a new version causing invalid state transitions
relevant_fix_clues: No bugs currently show resolved status or explicit fixes

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crashes stem from buffer management issues introduced or exacerbated by the neqo update to v0.22.0. The crash signature in get_output_buffer points to invalid memory access likely due to race conditions or improper synchronization during buffer handling. No resolved bugs or fixes are currently available to confirm remediation strategies.

refined_analysis.root_cause: Upgrading neqo QUIC from pre-0.22.0 to v0.22.x introduced stricter lint rules causing CI failures and concurrency bugs in get_output_buffer() leading to memory corruption.
symptoms: CI linting failures after neqo v0.22.x update
Use-after-free and out-of-memory crashes in netwerk/neqo/src/connection.rs get_output_buffer() under concurrent QUIC traffic
reproduction_steps: Update neqo dependency in netwerk/neqo/ to v0.22.x
Run linting and unit tests to confirm lint failures
Generate concurrent QUIC traffic to trigger crashes in get_output_buffer()
refined_analysis.technical_details: neqo v0.22.x introduced stricter linting fixed in v0.22.2, but get_output_buffer() in connection.rs has race conditions or missing synchronization around output buffer lifecycle, causing use-after-free and OOM crashes during parallel QUIC operations.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Blocks CI due to lint errors and causes runtime crashes in networking features relying on neqo QUIC.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Add synchronization (e.g., mutexes or atomic references) in get_output_buffer() within netwerk/neqo/src/connection.rs to protect buffer allocation and lifecycle. Fix lint errors introduced by neqo v0.22.x to restore CI. Validate with unit and concurrency stress tests simulating parallel QUIC traffic.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Updating netwerk/neqo to neqo v0.22.x causes CI lint failures and concurrency bugs in get_output_buffer() (netwerk/neqo/src/connection.rs) resulting in memory corruption and crashes. The fix requires adding synchronization to output buffer management and resolving lint errors to stabilize build and runtime.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis accurately identifies the core issue and affected areas but is overly verbose and includes distracting details. It lacks precise, actionable reproduction steps and explicit function signatures or code snippets to guide implementation. The 'proposed_fix_approach' is vague and does not specify what synchronization primitives or mechanisms to use or reference existing patterns in the codebase. The analysis is not fully self-contained, assuming familiarity with neqo versions and lint fixes without linking or embedding them. Overall, it needs to be more concise, tightly scoped, and provide clearer, file-level guidance for AI to generate a merge-ready fix.

filtered_context.concise_summary: Updating netwerk/neqo to neqo v0.22.x causes CI lint failures and race conditions in get_output_buffer() (netwerk/neqo/src/connection.rs), leading to use-after-free and memory corruption under concurrent QUIC traffic. Fix requires applying synchronization (e.g., mutex or atomic reference counting) around output buffer management in get_output_buffer() and resolving lint errors introduced in neqo v0.22.x to restore CI and prevent crashes.
core_symptoms: CI lint failures after updating neqo to v0.22.x
Use-after-free and out-of-memory crashes in get_output_buffer() under concurrent QUIC operations
technical_constraints: Add synchronization primitives to protect output buffer lifecycle in get_output_buffer()
Fix stricter linting errors introduced in neqo v0.22.x to pass CI
Validate fix with concurrency stress tests simulating parallel QUIC traffic
